title: ranunculus peltatus subsp. baudotii
intro: Ranunculus peltatus subsp. baudotii, also known as water crowfoot, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and reducing swelling. The health benefits of water crowfoot include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to promote healing and well-being.
